question_text: An electromagnet with 100 coils uses 3 amperes of current and has a magnetic field strength of 60 units. What would the magnetic field strength be if the current were reduced to 1 ampere, assuming all other factors stay the same?
explanation: The magnetic field strength is directly proportional to the current. If the current is reduced to 1/3 of its original value (from 3A to 1A), the magnetic field strength is also reduced to 1/3 of its original value (60 divided by 3 equals 20 units).
